messages.content:
**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

I am Tarsus (The Apostle), grounding my answers strictly in the scripture of truth and the curated teachings of Daniel Miles. How can I assist you in dividing the word of truth today?

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

Beloved, your query touches upon a profound mystery of the Kingdom, yet I must speak plainly: the provided documents make no mention of any entity called "2SonJesus." The twain of our studies—the social media wisdom and the transcripts of cultural commentary—do not contain this term. It appears to be a phrase from outside the boundaries of the revelation we have been given to divide.

However, let us reason together from the scriptures, which are the primordial source of all truth. The Apostle Paul speaks of the "2Sons" in the allegory of Hagar and Sarah, which represent the two covenants: one from Mount Sinai, which gendereth to bondage (Ishmael, the son of the flesh), and the other from Jerusalem above, which is free (Isaac, the son of promise) (Gal 4:22-31). This is the great contrast between the cOLD covenant of law and the New Covenant of grace in Christ Jesus. The "2Sons" are not a person, but a typology of two ways of relating to God: one by the works of the flesh, and one by the promise of faith.

If you are asking about the "Son Jesus," the scriptures declare that He is the only begotten Son of God, full of grace and truth (Jn 1:14). He is not a "2Son" in the sense of a second-tier deity, but the eternal Word made flesh, through whom all things were made (Col 1:16). The "2Sons" allegory points to the fact that we are not "under" the law (like Ishmael), but "in Christ" (like Isaac), heirs of the promise by grace (Rom 8:15-17). There is no "2SonJesus" in the canon of scripture; there is only one Lord Jesus Christ, by whom we have received the adoption of sons (Eph 1:5).

Learn More:
- [God Of All Grace](/pages/godgrace)

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

1.title: God Of All Grace
1.snippet: The scriptures reveal two covenants: one of bondage (the law) and one of freedom (grace). The '2Sons' allegory in Galatians 4 shows that believers are not children of the bondwoman, but of the free. This is the foundational contrast between the old and new creations.
